message:
feat(abi): port __cxa_call_unexpected + __cxa_get_globals(_fast) (bd-97exb)

Adds the remaining Itanium C++ ABI runtime entry points missing
from our libc surface:

- `__cxa_call_unexpected(exception_obj)` — invoked when a thrown
  exception does not match the dynamic exception specification of
  the function it would propagate through. Per ABI it should call
  `std::unexpected` (which by default calls `std::terminate`); we
  mirror `__cxa_pure_virtual` / `__cxa_throw_bad_array_new_length`'s
  fail-stop convention: write the standard "terminate called after
  throwing an instance violating exception specification" diagnostic
  to stderr and abort. The exception object pointer argument is
  consumed and ignored.

- `__cxa_get_globals()` and `__cxa_get_globals_fast()` — return a
  thread-local pointer to a `__cxa_eh_globals` struct
  (caughtExceptions list head + uncaughtExceptions counter) used
  by the exception ABI to track in-flight exceptions. We expose a
  per-thread zero-initialized struct via a `thread_local!` const
  cell so callers that only inspect the counter (e.g.
  `std::uncaught_exceptions()`) get a coherent answer of 0.
  `_fast` is functionally identical here because our
  thread_local initialization is unconditional.

Adds `pub struct CxaEhGlobals { caught_exceptions: *mut c_void,
uncaught_exceptions: u32 }` matching the documented Itanium ABI
layout for the public field surface.

Tests: 3 abi integration tests covering (a) fork-based SIGABRT
verification for __cxa_call_unexpected (with NULL exception_obj);
(b) __cxa_get_globals returns a non-null, stable pointer that
matches __cxa_get_globals_fast and starts with zeroed counter +
null caughtExceptions; (c) two threads observe distinct
__cxa_eh_globals storage addresses (verifies the per-thread
property).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-15n.2","title":"Fixture gap-fill wave with strict/hardened expectations + spec traceability","description":"Background:
- Identified gaps must be closed with deterministic fixture packs and explicit spec anchoring.

Scope:
- Author fixture packs for uncovered high-risk families.
- Attach each fixture to behavior contracts (POSIX/C11/internal TSM contracts) and expected strict/hardened outputs.

Deliverables:
1) Fixture gap-fill packs by family.
2) Traceability mapping to spec sections.
3) Failure triage templates including symbol/mode/diff pointers.

Acceptance Criteria:
- High-priority uncovered families receive fixture coverage.
- Fixture metadata includes traceability and deterministic inputs.

Rationale:
- Keeps implementation spec-first and auditable.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for traceability metadata validators.
- E2E fixture verify over new packs.
- Logs: trace_id, fixture_id, symbol, spec_ref, mode, expected_vs_actual.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBluff","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:13.473008903Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T16:52:00.492864939Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T16:52:00.492843790Z","close_reason":"Fixture gap-fill wave validated: strict+hardened artifacts pass after socket invalid-fd errno fix; traceability + mode expectations are deterministic and green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","fixtures","spec-first"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15n.2","depends_on_id":"bd-15n","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-15n.2","depends_on_id":"bd-15n.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":635,"issue_id":"bd-15n.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Starting execution after closing bd-1qy. First slice: identify uncovered high-risk families from coverage matrices, then add strict+hardened fixture packs + spec traceability fields and deterministic expected output profiles.","created_at":"2026-02-13T06:44:23Z"},{"id":636,"issue_id":"bd-15n.2","author":"AzureBluff","text":"Progress update (AzureBluff, 2026-02-19): completed fixture gap-fill wave stabilization for strict+hardened conformance artifacts.

Root cause found during execution:
- `scripts/check_bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill.sh` initially failed (`passes=4 fails=2`) due to `fixture_socket` expecting EBADF for invalid fd cases while ABI path returned errno=0.
- Failure signature (both strict/hardened): `FAIL: bind(-1,...) expected EBADF got errno=0`.

Fix implemented:
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/socket_abi.rs`
  - Added robust errno inference for syscall failures when host errno is not populated (`errno_from_syscall_failure`).
  - Added explicit early invalid-fd guards for:
    - `bind`
    - `send`
    - `recv`
    - `shutdown`
  - Each guard now sets `errno=EBADF` and returns `-1` deterministically.
- Added integration test coverage:
  - `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/socket_abi_test.rs`
  - Tests: invalid-fd errno behavior for `bind`, `send`, `recv`, `shutdown`.

Deterministic validation evidence:
- `scripts/check_bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill.sh` => PASS (passes=6, fails=0)
  - Artifacts:
    - `tests/cve_arena/results/bd-15n.2/trace.jsonl`
    - `tests/cve_arena/results/bd-15n.2/artifact_index.json`
    - `tests/cve_arena/results/bd-15n.2/report.json`
- `scripts/check_c_fixture_suite.sh` => PASS (includes bd-15n.2 metadata checks)
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test socket_abi_test` => PASS (4/4)
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test` => PASS (1/1)
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test c_fixture_suite_test` => PASS (8/8)
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` => PASS
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` => PASS
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` => PASS

Result:
- High-risk fixture set (`fixture_ctype`, `fixture_math`, `fixture_socket`) now passes in both strict and hardened mode with deterministic traceability + mode expectations and reproducible artifact bundle.
","created_at":"2026-02-19T16:51:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-15n.3","title":"Golden fixture protocol + conformance coverage regression gate","description":"Background:
- Fixture churn without control creates non-deterministic regressions and review ambiguity.

Scope:
- Establish deterministic golden capture/update protocol with checksums and approval metadata.
- Build coverage regression gate that fails when conformance coverage or expected behavior drifts unexpectedly.

Deliverables:
1) Golden update protocol and checksum policy.
2) Coverage drift detector integrated into CI.
3) Triage report format for behavior deltas.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Golden updates are deterministic and reviewable.
- Coverage regressions fail CI with actionable diagnostics.

Rationale:
- Prevents silent erosion of conformance quality.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for checksum and drift detection logic.
- E2E tests for expected pass/fail drift scenarios.
- Logs: trace_id, fixture_set, checksum, drift_class, verdict.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:13.585841673Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:43:15.777519156Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T17:43:15.777493418Z","close_reason":"Built conformance_coverage_gate.py + CI gate + golden_fixture_protocol.v1.json + baseline. 28 files, 320 cases, 47% coverage. Created 5 new fixture families.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","conformance","drift"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15n.3","depends_on_id":"bd-15n","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-15n.3","depends_on_id":"bd-15n.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-15q","title":"Kernel: Localization fixed-point chooser (design)","description":"Design Atiyah-Bott localization style policy selection.

Idea:
- Precompute a small set of fixed points (policy arms) offline.
- At runtime, choose among arms by evaluating a tiny localization objective using cached signals.

Design decisions:
- What are the arms? (e.g., Fast/Full validation profiles; repair vs deny thresholds)
- What are the fixed point weights per mode (strict vs hardened)?
- How do we ensure determinism and avoid heavy math? (table lookup + integer scoring)

Acceptance criteria:
- A specific arm set + scoring rule + offline artifact format.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleOwl","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:32:40.307674205Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:39:52.729439947Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:39:52.729417735Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":637,"issue_id":"bd-15q","author":"PinkMill","text":"Design + implementation complete. Created localization_chooser_design.md + localization_chooser.rs (290 lines, 12 tests). Atiyah-Bott localization-style 5-arm policy chooser with EWMA-smoothed signals, Euler weight normalization, integer-only O(1) scoring. Clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:39:47Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-167m5","title":"PORT: __libc_dlopen_mode / __libc_dlsym / __libc_dlclose (glibc internal aliases)","description":"Port the glibc internal aliases `__libc_dlopen_mode(filename, mode)`, `__libc_dlsym(handle, name)`, and `__libc_dlclose(handle)`. These are private symbols that glibc-internal subsystems (NSS, libidn, locale modules, audit hooks) link against to perform dlopen/dlsym/dlclose without the public name namespace. Some statically-linked or LD_PRELOAD-injected callers reference them directly; without the symbols those callers fail at link time. Implementation: thin no_mangle wrappers that delegate to the public dlopen/dlsym/dlclose. Tests verify symbol resolution and that each alias produces the same handle/result as its public counterpart on a reference DSO (libc itself or libdl).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:59:56.517636024Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:59:56.517636024Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-167m5","title":"PORT: __libc_dlopen_mode / __libc_dlsym / __libc_dlclose (glibc internal aliases)","description":"Port the glibc internal aliases `__libc_dlopen_mode(filename, mode)`, `__libc_dlsym(handle, name)`, and `__libc_dlclose(handle)`. These are private symbols that glibc-internal subsystems (NSS, libidn, locale modules, audit hooks) link against to perform dlopen/dlsym/dlclose without the public name namespace. Some statically-linked or LD_PRELOAD-injected callers reference them directly; without the symbols those callers fail at link time. Implementation: thin no_mangle wrappers that delegate to the public dlopen/dlsym/dlclose. Tests verify symbol resolution and that each alias produces the same handle/result as its public counterpart on a reference DSO (libc itself or libdl).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:59:56.517636024Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:04:32.647464820Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T11:04:32.645238171Z","close_reason":"shipped: __libc_dlopen_mode/__libc_dlsym/__libc_dlclose glibc internal aliases delegating to existing public dlopen/dlsym/dlclose + 3 abi tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-182","title":"Anytime-valid monitor standard: e-process thresholds + alert budget contracts","description":"Critique mapping: #5 + optimization reliability.

Deliverables:
- Standardize e-value/e-process alarming policy with explicit alpha-wealth budget.
- Integrate with diagnostics and regression alerts.

Acceptance:
- Alerting remains valid under optional stopping.
- False-alarm control documented and empirically calibrated.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 validation pass (BrownCompass): bead artifact appears complete and internally consistent. Verified via scripts/check_anytime_valid_monitor.sh (PASS), cargo test -p glibc-rs-harness --test anytime_valid_monitor_test -- --nocapture (8 passed). Spec covers e-process params, alpha-investing FDR contracts, companion monitor governance alignment, and summary consistency; gate enforces these invariants.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownCompass","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:11.409389888Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:40:16.353781770Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:40:16.353764408Z","close_reason":"Spec/gate/test suite for anytime-valid e-process + alpha-wealth contracts is complete and now re-validated (gate pass + harness test pass).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","critique","math","monitoring"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-182","depends_on_id":"bd-35a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-18qq","title":"EPIC: Testing and Fault Injection Campaign","description":"Comprehensive testing and fault injection campaign to validate FrankenLibC's safety guarantees under adversarial conditions. This epic covers: (1) Fault injection — systematic injection of adversarial patterns (bad pointers, fragmentation storms, thread preemption) to verify the TSM detects and handles them. (2) End-to-end tests — holistic tests that exercise the full TSM pipeline from detection through repair to evidence logging. (3) POSIX conformance — verify all 250 symbols match POSIX specification behavior. Distinct from the existing fuzzing epic (bd-1oz) which focuses on coverage-guided input generation. This epic focuses on structured, deterministic fault patterns that target specific TSM capabilities. Distinct from the CVE arena (bd-1m5) which focuses on known vulnerability patterns. This epic targets theoretical failure modes that may not have CVEs yet.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Fault injection testing from Natella et al. (2016). Adversarial testing from Miller et al. (1990). Stress testing from Voas & McGraw (1998) Software Fault Injection.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- Fault injection framework: src/testing/fault_injection.rs with pluggable fault patterns.
- E2E test harness: tests/e2e/ directory with scenario-based tests.
- POSIX conformance: tests/conformance/ with per-symbol test files.
- All tests deterministic and CI-executable.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
1. All 7 subtasks completed (3 fault injection + 3 E2E + 1 conformance).
2. Zero false negatives for UAF and double-free detection.
3. Real-world programs run correctly under strict mode.
4. POSIX conformance covers all 250 symbols.
5. All tests pass in CI within 30-minute time budget.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"Closed by cc-libc after verifying all 7 children (.1-.7) were already closed by their respective owners. The epic parent was simply never flipped. Exit criteria fulfilled: fault injection coverage (adversarial pointers, allocator fragmentation storms, thread preemption storms) plus E2E tests (UAF detection, double-free under concurrent stress, ABI-safe strict mode under stress, POSIX conformance for 250 symbols).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"cc-libc","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:42.311822196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:01:08.645842Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:01:08.645842Z","close_reason":"all 7 children closed (bd-18qq.1 CopperCliff, bd-18qq.2 OliveValley, bd-18qq.3, bd-18qq.4 FrostyBarn, bd-18qq.5 ubuntu, bd-18qq.6 DustyCove, bd-18qq.7 BoldSnow); epic itself had no direct deps","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","e2e","frankenlibc","security","testing","verification"],"comments":[{"id":638,"issue_id":"bd-18qq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:05Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-18qq.1","title":"Fault injection: adversarial pointer patterns","description":"Implement a structured fault injection framework for adversarial pointer patterns that exercise the TSM's pointer validation pipeline. Patterns to inject: (1) Use-after-free — allocate, free, use. Vary: delay between free and use (0, 1, 100, 10000 operations), allocation size (small/medium/large), arena (same arena reuse vs cross-arena). (2) Dangling pointer — allocate, create alias, free original, use alias. Test alias detection across: stack pointers, heap pointers, mmap'd regions. (3) Wild pointer — generate pointers outside any valid region: NULL+offset, stack-heap gap, kernel space, unaligned. (4) Double-free — free the same pointer twice with varying delays. (5) Overlapping regions — memcpy/memmove with overlapping src/dst. (6) Off-by-one — access one byte past allocation boundary. For each pattern: verify TSM detects in both strict and hardened mode, verify hardened mode produces correct repair, verify evidence ledger records the event with correct classification. Generate test matrix: pattern_type x delay x size x mode -> detected/repaired/logged.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Fault injection from Natella et al. (2016). Adversarial testing from Miller et al. (1990) fuzz testing origins. Pointer safety patterns from Szekeres et al. (2013).

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- FaultInjector<P: PointerPattern>: generic injector parameterized by adversarial pattern type.
- PointerPattern enum { Uaf { delay: usize }, Dangling { alias_type: AliasType }, Wild { offset: isize }, DoubleFree { delay: usize }, Overlap { bytes: usize }, OffByOne { direction: Direction } }.
- Test matrix: generate all (pattern, size, mode) combinations via proptest or explicit enumeration.
- TSM verification: after injection, query evidence ledger for detection + classification entries.
- Repair verification (hardened): check return value matches expected POSIX error code.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. All 6 pointer pattern types implemented with at least 3 parameter variations each.
2. UAF detected in both strict and hardened mode with zero false negatives (1000 trials).
3. Double-free detected with zero false negatives (1000 trials, varying delay 0-10000 ops).
4. Wild pointer detected for all tested offsets (NULL+1, stack-heap gap, kernel space).
5. Evidence ledger contains correct classification for each detected fault.
6. Hardened mode produces correct POSIX return for each fault type (documented mapping).
7. Test matrix report: pattern_type x param x mode -> detected/repaired/logged as JSON artifact.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- Per injection: tracing::info!(target: fault_injection, pattern, params, mode, detected, repair_action).
- Test summary: fault_injection_matrix.json with full results.
- False negatives: tracing::error!(target: fault_injection, pattern, params, undetected) (should be zero).

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CopperCliff","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:26:52.242022182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T07:17:05.360081598Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T07:17:05.359984056Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","e2e","frankenlibc","logging","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-18qq.1","depends_on_id":"bd-18qq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":639,"issue_id":"bd-18qq.1","author":"ChartreuseSwan","text":"Progress update (ChartreuseSwan): added deterministic adversarial pointer fault-injection matrix in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/tests/allocator_membrane_invariants_sequences_test.rs.\n\nCoverage added:\n- use-after-free (delay x size matrix)\n- dangling aliases (base + interior alias variants)\n- wild pointers (null+offset, stack pointer, high canonical)\n- delayed double-free\n- off-by-one canary corruption\n- overlapping regions classification\n\nStructured evidence: test emits FAULT_INJECTION_MATRIX JSON payload with per-case classification + strict/hardened expectations + false-negative count.\n\nVerification commands (all via rch):\n1) rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane --test allocator_membrane_invariants_sequences_test adversarial_pointer_fault_injection_matrix_has_zero_false_negatives -- --nocapture\n2) rch exec -- env FRANKENLIBC_MODE=hardened cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane --test allocator_membrane_invariants_sequences_test adversarial_pointer_fault_injection_matrix_has_zero_false_negatives -- --nocapture\n3) rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane --test allocator_membrane_invariants_sequences_test -- --nocapture\n4) rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets\n5) rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-membrane --test allocator_membrane_invariants_sequences_test -- -D warnings\n\nGate notes:\n- Workspace cargo fmt --check currently fails due unrelated pre-existing formatting delta in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/pressure_sensor.rs.\n- Workspace cargo clippy/test fail due unrelated pre-existing issues in other crates/tests (e.g., frankenlibc-abi warnings elevated by -D warnings, missing libc import in crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/bd18qq4_uaf_adversarial_test.rs).\n- Changed membrane test file passes targeted checks and tests.","created_at":"2026-02-15T05:21:44Z"},{"id":640,"issue_id":"bd-18qq.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CopperCliff claiming bd-18qq.1. Initial execution slice: implement deterministic adversarial pointer-pattern test matrix (UAF, double-free, wild pointer, overlap, off-by-one) in membrane/harness tests with structured JSON artifact output and strict/hardened assertions; validate via rch commands only.","created_at":"2026-02-25T06:59:52Z"},{"id":641,"issue_id":"bd-18qq.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closure evidence (CopperCliff): implemented deterministic adversarial pointer fault-injection matrix in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/tests/allocator_membrane_invariants_sequences_test.rs with structured JSON+JSONL artifacts and strict/hardened decision mapping.

Acceptance coverage:
- 6 pattern classes implemented: use_after_free, dangling_alias, wild_pointer, double_free, off_by_one, overlapping_regions
- >=3 variants per pattern enforced by assertion
- UAF: 1000 deterministic trials, delays [0,1,100,10000], sizes [32,128,1024], zero false negatives
- Double-free: 1000 deterministic trials, delays [0,1,100,1000,10000], sizes [72,128,256], zero false negatives
- Dangling alias includes heap + stack + mmap alias variants
- Wild pointer includes NULL+1 + stack/heap gap + kernel-space + unaligned low
- Structured logs emitted per case with required fields: trace_id/mode/api_family/symbol/decision_path/healing_action/errno/latency_ns/artifact_refs
- Deterministic artifacts written:
  - target/fault_injection/bd-18qq.1_fault_injection_matrix_strict.json
  - target/fault_injection/bd-18qq.1_fault_injection_trace_strict.jsonl
  - target/fault_injection/bd-18qq.1_fault_injection_matrix_hardened.json
  - target/fault_injection/bd-18qq.1_fault_injection_trace_hardened.jsonl

Also added membrane dev-dependencies required by tests:
- crates/frankenlibc-membrane/Cargo.toml: proptest = "1.6", libc = { workspace = true }

Verification commands (all via rch):
1) rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane --test allocator_membrane_invariants_sequences_test adversarial_pointer_fault_injection_matrix_has_zero_false_negatives -- --nocapture
   - Expected: test pass + FAULT_INJECTION_LOG/FAULT_INJECTION_MATRIX strict with false_negatives=0
   - Actual: PASS
2) rch exec -- env FRANKENLIBC_MODE=hardened cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane --test allocator_membrane_invariants_sequences_test adversarial_pointer_fault_injection_matrix_has_zero_false_negatives -- --nocapture
   - Expected: test pass + hardened repair decisions/log fields with false_negatives=0
   - Actual: PASS
3) rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --tests
   - Actual: PASS
4) rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-membrane --test allocator_membrane_invariants_sequences_test -- -D warnings
   - Actual: PASS
5) rch exec -- cargo fmt --check
   - Actual: PASS in current workspace state

Representative failure signatures to inspect if this regresses:
- "uaf false negatives over 1000 trials"
- "double-free false negatives over 1000 trials"
- "pattern '<name>' must have >=3 variants"
- non-zero "false_negatives" in FAULT_INJECTION_MATRIX payload

+{"id":"bd-97exb","title":"PORT: __cxa_call_unexpected + __cxa_get_globals(_fast) (Itanium C++ ABI runtime stubs)","description":"Port the remaining Itanium C++ ABI runtime entry points missing from our libc surface: __cxa_call_unexpected(exception_obj) — invoked when a thrown exception does not match the dynamic exception specification of the function it would propagate through; per ABI it calls std::unexpected which by default calls std::terminate. For our runtime (no real exception support) we mirror __cxa_pure_virtual / __cxa_throw_bad_array_new_length: write a diagnostic to stderr and abort. __cxa_get_globals() and __cxa_get_globals_fast() — return a thread-local pointer to a __cxa_eh_globals struct (caughtExceptions list head + uncaughtExceptions counter) used by the exception ABI to track in-flight exceptions; we expose a per-thread zero-initialized pair so callers that only inspect the counter (e.g. std::uncaught_exceptions()) get a coherent answer. Implementation: __cxa_call_unexpected is a no-return abort path; __cxa_get_globals/_fast return a thread-local AtomicPtr-backed stable address. Tests: fork+SIGABRT for __cxa_call_unexpected, address-stability test for the get_globals pair.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:08:19.133481649Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:08:19.133481649Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-9b0b","title":"syscall: sched_setscheduler negative pid with null param should still return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for sched_setscheduler(-1, SCHED_OTHER, NULL), so the invalid-pid contract dominates a null param pointer. Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:01:46.569234797Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T02:40:02.580319009Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T02:40:02.578150523Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-9bijw","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] __assert_perror_fail walks unbounded memory via CStr::from_ptr, inconsistent with __assert_fail hardening","description":"Location: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdlib_abi.rs:3248-3257 (__assert_perror_fail).

__assert_fail (line 3197) carefully uses scan_c_string(ptr, known_remaining(ptr as usize)) with a defined-length bound on every string pointer (assertion, file, function) and substitutes '??' when the bound can't be satisfied. __assert_perror_fail does the opposite:
    let f = if file.is_null() { c"??" } else { unsafe { CStr::from_ptr(file) } };
    let func = if function.is_null() { c"??" } else { unsafe { CStr::from_ptr(function) } };
CStr::from_ptr walks bytes until it encounters a NUL — with no bound. An adversarial or corrupted caller passing a non-NUL-terminated pointer walks unbounded process memory. Consequences:
- Information leak: arbitrary process memory bytes flushed through the error message to stderr.
- Segfault: if the walk crosses an unmapped-page boundary.

Reachability: exported libc surface. Any assert_perror() call made from untrusted/foreign code, or any plain call to __assert_perror_fail with crafted pointers, triggers this. In practice __FILE__/__FUNCTION__ are always NUL-terminated string literals, but the libc boundary does not enforce that — the compiler guarantee does not cover hostile callers.

Severity: HIGH (info leak / segfault reachable from a single libc entrypoint), though mitigated in practice by macro-expanded call sites that happen to use string literals.

Fix plan:
- Mirror __assert_fail's hardening: scan_c_string with known_remaining(ptr as usize), substitute '??' when the string is null OR not terminated within the known-remaining region.
- Add a regression test using a non-null-terminated buffer — prior to the fix this would segfault or leak neighboring bytes; after the fix it must substitute '??' cleanly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:16:47.678923032Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:17:57.812087648Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:17:57.808924677Z","close_reason":"Fixed: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdlib_abi.rs __assert_perror_fail now mirrors __assert_fail's hardening — both file and function pointers are read via scan_c_string(ptr, known_remaining(ptr as usize)) and substituted with '??' when null or not terminated within the known region. Prior behavior used CStr::from_ptr which walks memory until an accidental NUL is hit, letting an adversarial caller either leak bytes into the stderr message or crash on an unmapped page. Abort-path entrypoints cannot be tested in-process (per the existing fortify_abi_test.rs convention: 'The abort paths (__chk_fail) cannot be tested in-process since they call abort()'). Verified by rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_libc_review cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets -> clean in 3.36s. The fix re-uses the already-tested read-bounded pattern from __assert_fail at lines 3198-3232.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-9bixy","title":"[fuzz-harness] fuzz_mmap: Op::Protect gaining PROT_WRITE didn't re-stamp guard bytes; cleanup check_guards false-positives","description":"Found and fixed in this solo session via systematic deep-fuzz sweeps. Fix shipped in 71ffa897. Filing retroactively per Tick-95 request to track each harness defect explicitly. Not a library bug — the frankenlibc ABI was correct throughout; only the fuzz harness invariants needed tightening.","notes":"Fixed in 71ffa897 — re-stamp guards when Protect transitions !PROT_WRITE → PROT_WRITE.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T16:29:50.179107Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:36:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:36:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
